features.title: Built to make studying faster
features.subtitle: Every feature in GenQuiZ is designed around one goal: turn any piece of text into a quiz you can take, review, and reuse — with as little friction as possible.

hero.title: How GenQuiZ Works
hero.subtitle: From raw text to a fully interactive quiz in seconds — here's the complete journey your content takes inside GenQuiZ.

flow.title: The Full Code Flow
flow.subtitle: Every step from input to export is handled transparently in the browser and through a serverless AWS Lambda — no data stored, no accounts needed.

step1.title: You paste your study material
step1.description: On the Generate Quiz page, you paste any raw text — lecture notes, a Wikipedia excerpt, a technical article. You also pick a difficulty level (Child → Hard) and how many questions you want (5–10).
step1.detail: DOMPurify sanitises the input in the browser before it ever leaves your device, stripping any unsafe HTML so only clean plain text is sent onward.

step2.title: Your text travels to AWS Lambda
step2.description: The app POSTs your content, difficulty level, and question count to an AWS API Gateway endpoint backed by a Node.js Lambda function.
step2.detail: The Lambda builds a strict system prompt instructing Amazon Nova Pro to produce questions in Aiken format — a plain-text MCQ standard used widely in e-learning. Token budget is calculated as (questionCount × 1200), capped at 10,000, ensuring the model has enough room without wasting resources.

step3.title: Amazon Nova Pro writes the questions
step3.description: AWS Bedrock invokes the amazon.nova-pro-v1:0 model with your text as the user message and the format instructions as a system prompt. The model returns questions in this exact structure: QUESTION → options A–D → ANSWER → EXPLANATION.
step3.detail: Inference parameters (temperature 0.7, topP 0.9, topK 20) are tuned for factual accuracy with a touch of creative phrasing — keeping questions clear without being robotic.

step4.title: Aiken format is parsed into structured data
step4.description: The raw model output is handed to extractFromAikenFormat() — a pure TypeScript parser that splits the text line-by-line, groups lines into question blocks by UUID, and maps each block into a typed question object with fields: question, options (keyed a–d), answer, and explanation.
step4.detail: The same parser powers the Load Quiz path — so whether AI generates the text or you paste it yourself, all downstream quiz logic is identical. The raw blocks are also preserved as Record<string, string[]> so you can copy or download the original Aiken text later.

step5.title: You take the quiz, then export what you want
step5.description: MCQMain renders one question at a time, tracking your selected options in global context. On submit, the Result page shows every question with colour-coded correct/wrong highlights and the AI's explanation.
step5.detail: You can cherry-pick questions using checkboxes, then copy the selected set to clipboard or download them as a .txt file — ready to paste into an LMS, share with classmates, or re-load into GenQuiZ another day.

paths.title: Two Ways to Start a Quiz

ai.title: AI-Powered Generation
ai.desc: Let GenQuiZ write the questions for you using Amazon Nova Pro via AWS Bedrock.
steps: Hit Generate — AI does the rest
Questions appear instantly, ready to attempt

parse.title: Load from Aiken Format
parse.desc: Already have questions written in Aiken format? Skip the AI step entirely.
steps: Paste Aiken-formatted text or upload a .txt / .md file
GenQuiZ parses the format locally — no network call
Questions load straight into quiz mode
Same result, export, and copy features apply

export.title: Your Results, Your Data
export.desc: After completing a quiz, the Result page gives you full control over the questions. Nothing is stored on a server — everything lives in your browser session.
